behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Signal rubric (MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP): 1/0/1/0/0. Failure boundary is module->API-client handoff (payload composition at call boundary), so Unit is too low for this behavior wording. Real network/OS is not required; a stub transport can catch missing labels. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about request shape, not model judgment or multi-step agent flow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound payload. Invoke classify(document, taxonomy) and assert every configured taxonomy label is present in the sent request, with no omissions.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Signal rubric: 1/0/1/0/0. This is response handoff + module policy enforcement (API adapter to caller contract), so Integration is the minimum realistic failure boundary. Rejected Agentic/System: behavior text focuses on deterministic post-processing of a returned value; no real model-quality or real network condition is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label (for example "medical"). Call classify and assert returned label is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Signal rubric: 0/0/0/0/0. This behavior is a pure normalization determinism property for identical inputs; no module interaction or I/O is required. Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly scopes to normalization step on identical raw responses.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object twice with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert outputs are identical (and optionally repeat across N runs).

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Signal rubric: 1/1/1/1/0. Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency of model/provider outputs over time. A mock would hide real instability, model drift, or nondeterministic classification. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision per call, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Against the real provider, issue repeated classify calls for fixed (document, taxonomy) under controlled prompt/settings; record raw provider responses and final labels. Assert final label is stable whenever raw provider response is unchanged; treat raw-response changes as allowed cause of label change.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Signal rubric: 1/0/1/0/0. The key failure is policy+builder+dispatch gating interaction (budget enforcement before send). Unit tests of tokenizer logic alone can miss send/no-send boundary bugs. Rejected System/Agentic: enforcement is pre-dispatch infrastructure logic; no real HTTP or model output is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small token budget and policy (truncate or reject), run classify with over-budget document using a fake client. Assert constructed request complies with budget and dispatch behavior matches policy (truncated payload sent, or request blocked with expected local error).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Signal rubric: 1/1/1/1/0. This depends on real provider token accounting returned by the AI API; stubs can mask mismatches between local estimator and provider metering. Per LLM-DEP rule, real AI/ML API execution path defaults to Agentic unless only transport plumbing is tested. Rejected Workflow: single-call metric check, not multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run classify against real provider for representative documents and capture provider-reported usage fields. Assert usage is <= configured budget for each case and flag regressions when provider metering exceeds budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Signal rubric: 1/0/1/0/0. Failure is retry/backoff orchestration across module and API client; an in-process fake returning rate-limit then success reproduces the real logic failure. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network timing primitives to validate correctness of wait+retry policy. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model output quality or chained model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client scripted to return rate-limit error (for example 429/Retry-After) then success. Assert classifier waits per policy (using controllable clock), retries expected number of times, and returns successful label instead of surfacing initial error.
